document.title: Risk Management Plan for ADD Digital flat panel X-ray detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC of 14 June 1993 as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC concerning medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector under EN ISO 14971:2019 and related medical device standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines risk management process, scope, responsibilities, risk acceptance criteria, and post-production information handling for ADD during its life cycle.
keyPoints: The plan applies risk management to the entire life cycle of ADD.
The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not mammography.
Risk level is calculated as Severity x Probability.
Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources, including PMCF, CAPA reports, complaints, recalls, scientific literature, and laws or standards trends.

concepts.name: Risk Management Plan
concepts.evidence: The purpose of risk management plan is applied EN 60601-1 3.1 Ed. according to EN ISO 14971:2019 by establish risk management process.

concepts.name: Risk Management Policy
concepts.evidence: According to the EN ISO 14971:2019 international standards of risk management we will be identified in advance and to control Hazard and Risk which may be caused by the product.

concepts.name: Risk Management Process
concepts.evidence: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

concepts.name: Intended Use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Post-production Information
concepts.evidence: Post-production Information should be collected through the collection methods such as surveys, customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaints (Internet, telephone, etc.), or articles or news reports.

concepts.name: Risk Control
concepts.evidence: risk control process in which decisions are made and measures implemented by which risks are reduced to, or maintained within, specified levels

concepts.name: Residual Risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk risk remaining after risk control measures have been taken

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Plan for ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for ADD Digital flat panel X-ray detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-036 Rev.2.

evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Quality team Han Jimin Dec 01, 2022; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Dec 01, 2022; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec 01, 2022

evidence.claim: Revision 2 was made according to the update of applicable standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 Dec 01, 2022 Revision according to the update of applicable standards.
Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The scope applies to the entire life cycle of ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: We shall apply to entire life cycle of the following product. Product Name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector Model name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities must be performed separately for each product and project risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management activities must be performed separately for each product and project risk

evidence.claim: Top management must ensure necessary risk management resources are available.
evidence.evidenceText: Representative Director shall ensure that the necessary resources are available when needed in risk management.

evidence.claim: The risk acceptability policy is based on applicable regulations, relevant international standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: this policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as Severity multiplied by Probability.
Risk levels 1 through 4 are in the acceptable green zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4

evidence.claim: Risk levels 5 through 25 are in the unacceptable red zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: All risks are reduced as far as possible without economic considerations.
evidence.evidenceText: All risks reduced “as far as possible” without economic considerations.

evidence.claim: Post-production information should be collected from internal and external information sources.
evidence.evidenceText: Post production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: Internal post-production sources include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production sources include medical incident reports, academic papers, similar device information, law and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards (5) In the process of performing clinical trials information

evidence.claim: Additional risk control measures can require changes to the risk management plan and risk management activities.
evidence.evidenceText: If it is determined that additional risk control measures are necessary in order to present the suitability evaluation factors should be identified or modified to perform additional changes to the risk management plan and risk management activities (risk analysis, risk assessment and risk control)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal metadata says FDA, but the source text references CE/MDD-related European directives and does not state FDA authority.
Manufacturer legal name is not present in the source text.
Some extracted text appears to have OCR or preprocessing quality issues, including merged words and misspellings such as 'this companyMore' and 'Severerity'.
